name: Source Fidelity review for Nad And Redox Restoration bundle
summary: The staged source, study, and finding records match the cited PubMed and trial-registry metadata closely enough for publication review. Titles, PMIDs, DOIs, registry IDs, intervention doses, populations, durations, and endpoint boundaries are consistent with the source records checked. The only source-fidelity caution is that the short GlyNAC dose-ranging study should remain explicitly framed as primary-endpoint-null with only post-hoc/subgroup glutathione support.

findings.claim_or_issue: The staged finding correctly treats the two-week GlyNAC dose-ranging study as mixed biomarker context, but the source's primary glutathione endpoints were not significantly improved versus placebo.
findings.why_it_matters: If this source were later summarized as a straightforward positive GlyNAC redox trial, it would overstate the primary result.
findings.recommended_action: Keep this finding as boundary/context support only; do not use it to upgrade functional or durable redox claims without the existing primary-endpoint-null caveat.

findings.claim_or_issue: The study sample_size value includes the young reference group, while the randomized older-adult intervention cohort was 117 enrolled and 114 completed.
findings.why_it_matters: Readers may otherwise interpret 134 as the randomized treatment cohort size.
findings.recommended_action: The staged quantitative note already preserves the cohort split; keep that note visible if the study record is displayed publicly.
